senses.definition: to give lessons to students in a university, college, school, etc.

examples.x: She teaches yoga to promote health and well-being.
The professor will teach a course on environmental science this semester.
I decided to leave my stressful job and teach painting at the community center.
He teaches math in high school and is very well-liked by students.
He taught mathematics at the local high school for ten years.

senses.definition: to teach a specific skill or a type of behavior to a person or an animal through a combination of instruction and practice over a period of time

examples.x: The coach regularly trains the team on new strategies for the game
Last month, she trained the employees on the use of the new software.
The circus performer trained the elephants for the upcoming show.
Right now, the instructor is actively training the recruits in physical fitness.
He is training new employees on how to use the company software.

senses.definition: to guide someone by providing information, training, or advice, helping them acquire new skills or understand a specific subject

examples.x: The experienced chef instructed the new apprentice in the art of gourmet cooking.
The piano teacher instructs her students in music theory and technique during their weekly lessons.
The yoga instructor carefully instructed the class in various poses to enhance flexibility and strength.
The martial arts instructor instructs the students in self-defense techniques.
The language tutor instructs her students in Spanish grammar and vocabulary

senses.definition: to act as the supervisor or teacher of someone less experienced

examples.x: The seasoned entrepreneur agreed to mentor the young startup founder, offering insights and advice.
As part of the internship program, senior employees volunteered to mentor new hires in the company culture and procedures.
She decided to mentor aspiring writers, providing constructive feedback and guidance on their work.
The veteran musician offered to mentor the talented young singer, sharing knowledge about the music industry and performance techniques.

senses.definition: to give information about someone or something, especially in an official manner

examples.x: The teacher informed the students about the upcoming exam schedule and its format.
The news anchor will inform the public of the latest developments in the ongoing investigation.
The manager informed the team of the changes in the project timeline to keep everyone updated.
The sign in the park informs visitors about the rules and regulations for maintaining a clean environment.
The doctor took the time to inform the patient of the potential side effects of the prescribed medication.

senses.definition: to teach someone, often within a school or university setting

examples.x: Schools exist to educate children.
The university aims to educate future leaders.
Teachers work hard to educate their students.
The program helps educate young people about health.
She was educated at a prestigious university.

senses.definition: to become knowledgeable or skilled in something by doing it, studying, or being taught

examples.x: He learned valuable negotiation skills by watching experienced negotiators in action
I learned to ride a bicycle when I was a child.
They are learning about history in their school lessons.
I'm eager to learn about different cultures.
We need to learn how to manage our time better.

senses.definition: to spend time to learn about certain subjects by reading books, going to school, etc.

examples.x: She spends hours every day to study for her upcoming exams.
He prefers to study in the library where it's quiet and conducive to learning.
They are studying for the science competition next month.
We study together to prepare for our group presentation.
She studied the history of art for her final paper.

senses.definition: to do or play something many times to become good at it

examples.x: Musicians regularly practice their instruments to improve their skills.
Athletes diligently practice their routines to enhance performance.
In preparation for the upcoming exam, students are advised to practice solving a variety of questions and problems.
Before the big game, the basketball team gathered to practice offensive and defensive strategies on the court.
The tennis player practiced serving and volleying for hours to refine their game before the tournament.

senses.definition: to engage in intense and concentrated studying within a limited timeframe, typically right before an examination

examples.x: As the final exams approached, Sarah had to cram all night to catch up on the semester's material.
Feeling unprepared, Jack decided to cram for his history test the night before.
Mark spent the entire weekend cramming for the certification exam.
Rather than spacing out his studying over several weeks, Tom opted to cram for his science exam.

senses.definition: to study intensively in preparation for an exam

examples.x: The library was packed with students grinding for their midterms.
They will grind late into the night to ensure they're ready.
He knew he had to grind hard over the weekend to pass his upcoming physics exam.
He decided to skip the party and spend the night grinding for his calculus test instead.

senses.definition: to repeat something until it is kept in one's memory

examples.x: Students often memorize multiplication tables to enhance their math skills.
Actors may memorize lines for a play through consistent rehearsal.
Language learners strive to memorize vocabulary to improve fluency.
Last week, he successfully memorized the entire poem for the recitation.
Musicians practice to memorize sheet music for a flawless performance.

senses.definition: to copy intellectual material without permission or proper attribution

examples.x: The student was caught cribbing from online sources for their research paper.
It's important to cite your sources properly to avoid cribbing in academic writing.
The company faced a lawsuit for cribbing design elements from a smaller competitor's product.

senses.definition: to participate in a specific examination or assessment

examples.x: Students were required to take a comprehensive final exam at the end of the semester.
She decided to take the proficiency test to demonstrate her language skills.
In high school, I had to take the SAT as part of the college admissions process.
Candidates must take a series of aptitude tests as part of the job application.
Before starting the new job, employees are often asked to take a skills assessment.
I will need to take the bar exam to practice law in this state.

senses.definition: to take an exam again after an initial attempt, typically to improve one's score or performance

examples.x: She retakes the exam next week to improve her grade.
They have retaken the assessment multiple times to meet the passing requirement.
We will retake the quiz after studying more thoroughly.
The students are retaking the exam now, hoping for better results.

senses.definition: to give a score to a student's performance

examples.x: The teacher spent the weekend grading her students' essays.
She was nervous about how the professor would grade her final project.
He is known to grade fairly but with high expectations.
After grading the tests, the instructor returned them to the students with feedback.
The professor explained the criteria she would use to grade the assignments.

senses.definition: to get the necessary grades in an exam, test, course, etc.

examples.x: Did you pass all your exams?
He hasn‚Äôt passed his driving test yet.
I'm not really expecting to pass first time.
She passed with flying colors.
I barely passed that test, it was so hard!

senses.definition: to perform extremely well in something, especially a test

examples.x: Despite the difficult questions, she managed to ace the final exam with a perfect score.
The dedicated student studied diligently and was able to ace the challenging physics test.
He consistently aces his presentations by thoroughly preparing and delivering with confidence.
The athlete trained rigorously and managed to ace the competition, winning a gold medal.
With focused preparation, the job candidate aced the interview and secured the position.

senses.definition: to fail in reaching the required standard to succeed in a test, course of study, etc.

examples.x: Despite studying hard, he unfortunately flunked the math exam.
If students don't complete their assignments, they may flunk the course.
Without proper preparation, it's easy to flunk a challenging subject in school.
The lack of attendance and effort caused several students to flunk the class.
Failing to submit the project on time could lead to a decision to flunk the course.

senses.definition: to lower a score or assessment given to someone in an exam, etc. due to errors or shortcomings

examples.x: The teacher marked down his essay for poor grammar.
The student's score was marked down for not answering all of the questions.
The project was marked down for not meeting the required criteria.
The judge marked down the gymnast's routine for a missed landing.

senses.definition: to study a subject carefully and systematically to discover new facts or information about it

examples.x: Before buying a new laptop, I researched different brands and models.
Sarah is researching healthy recipes to improve her cooking skills.
She researched various travel destinations before planning her vacation.
The professor is researching the impact of climate change on marine life.
The students researched different sources for their science project.

senses.definition: to examine something closely and carefully in order to find errors

examples.x: Inspectors scrutinized the company's financial records to find any errors.
The editor scrutinized the text to ensure it was free of any grammatical errors.
Software specialists scrutinized the code to find any existing bugs.
Opponents scrutinized the new bill to find its flaws.
The customs officer scrutinized the passenger's suitcase to ensure they weren't carrying any contraband.

senses.definition: to enter one's name in a list of an institute, school, etc.

examples.x: She decided to register for the online course to improve her skills.
You need to register at the university‚Äôs office before attending classes.
He failed to register in time for the new semester and had to wait until next year.
She plans to register for the science club at the start of the school year.

senses.definition: to officially enroll or register as a student at a school, college, or university

examples.x: She plans to matriculate at Harvard University in the fall.
He will matriculate in the engineering program next semester.
International students must meet specific requirements to matriculate in U.S. universities.
The university offers a special program for students who matriculate in January.
She intends to matriculate at a medical school after completing her bachelor's degree.

senses.definition: to study a particular subject as one's main field of study at a college or university

examples.x: I am majoring in computer science because I want to be a software engineer.
He plans to major in Computer Science and develop software applications.
They are both majoring in business because they want to start their own companies.
I majored in English at Stanford University.

senses.definition: to give an official degree, title, right, etc. to someone

examples.x: The university will confer degrees upon the graduating students during the commencement ceremony.
The monarch will confer a knighthood upon the distinguished individual for their service to the country.
The board decided to confer the prestigious award on the researcher for their groundbreaking contributions.
The organization regularly confers certifications on professionals who meet the standards.
The university conferred a Bachelor's degree on the graduating students.

senses.definition: to deliberately absent oneself from a class or school activity without permission

examples.x: He decided to ditch school and spend the day hanging out with his friends instead.
Despite knowing the consequences, she chose to ditch class to attend a concert downtown.
The students regularly ditched their first-period class to avoid a particularly challenging subject.
He regretted ditching the important review session before the exam and struggled with the material as a result.

senses.definition: to attend a class or course for personal enrichment or review without receiving academic credit

examples.x: Despite already graduating, she decided to audit the advanced physics course for her own interest.
He chose to audit the history seminar to deepen his understanding of the subject.
The university allows alumni to audit select courses free of charge as part of its lifelong learning program.
She audited the literature class to refresh her knowledge before starting her graduate studies.
They will audit the economics seminar next semester to broaden their knowledge of financial markets.

senses.definition: to avoid attending an event

examples.x: She decided to skip out on the company meeting to avoid a lengthy discussion.
Instead of finishing the entire workshop, he chose to skip out after the first hour.
The students planned to skip out on the lecture and enjoy the sunny weather outside.
Feeling uninterested, he decided to skip out on the mandatory training session at work.
They made a pact to skip out on the family gathering and spend the weekend on their own.
